titleZh: 酒店要被 AI 推荐，先把这五类公开资料补齐
titleEn: For Hotels to Be Recommended by AI, First Complete These Five Categories of Public Information
summaryZh: 酒店出现在AI推荐列表，从来不是运气。背后是公开信息被算法理解、匹配、排序的结果。补齐目标客群、房型服务、交通周边、企业会议资料和真实案例这五类信息，AI就能读懂你、推准你。多一份结构化资料，就多一条被选中的路径。
summaryEn: Hotels are not recommended by AI because they lack advertising copy. They are recommended when their public information lets the system and the customer judge who the hotel is for, what needs it solves, and why it is worth choosing. The first step is not writing more ads. It is completing five categories of public information: target guests, room and service scenarios, transport and surroundings, corporate client capability, and verified reviews.
contentZh:
酒店要被 AI 推荐，先把这五类公开资料补齐
迈创兄弟C&T（MarvelBros C&T）
酒店出现在AI推荐列表，从来不是运气。背后是公开信息被算法理解、匹配、排序的结果。补齐目标客群、房型服务、交通周边、企业会议资料和真实案例这五类信息，AI就能读懂你、推准你。多一份结构化资料，就多一条被选中的路径。
某山水度假酒店在OTA上口碑良好，可连续数月无缘AI行程规划工具的推荐。管理方发现，官网全是大片风景与感性文案，第三方平台的企业栏空白，地图上无会议厅标签，交通指引只写了一句“交通便利”。当用户问AI“带老人去安静度假的酒店”，算法无法识别这里有陪护服务与无障碍房；当行政专员搜“周边有高铁站的团建酒店”，系统抓取不到任何对应设施。信息缺口直接制造了需求盲区。酒店没输在硬件上，却输在了AI无法理解它。
资料一：目标客群
公开信息要刻画出具体的客群轮廓。放弃“面向中高端人群”的模糊表述，改用亲子家庭、商务散客、蜜月情侣、康养银发等可标签化的词语。AI在解析查询意图时，会做用户画像与酒店标签的语义对齐。某温泉酒店在官网、百科词条和本地攻略中持续强化“情侣泡汤”“结婚纪念日”等场景描述后，在“浪漫旅行推荐”类提问中的被提及频次明显提高。写客群描述时嵌入具体的需求动词，如“为孩童提供挖沙玩具和看护”“为商务客人单设快捷退房通道”，算法就能把这些短句转化为可检索的结构化标签，织入推荐的知识网络。
资料二：房型与服务
每种房型都需要独立成段，写出面积、床型、朝向、景观、设施，并直接关联可享受的服务。别只写“豪华海景房”，要写“180度正海景，2米特大床，配备戴森吹风机，含双早与迷你吧，可加婴儿床”。AI会把这类细节切分成特征向量，与用户提问中的“带婴儿床”“海景浴缸”等高意向词做精确匹配。服务介绍同样要拆解成高密度的关键词短语，如“24小时管家”“免费机场接驳”“宠物友好套房可接待中型犬”。某城市精品酒店把房型与服务做结构化梳理，更新至官网和OTA底层信息后，由AI入口导流的订单从零星跃升为稳定增量。客房的每一个参数，都是算法推荐的理由。
资料三：交通与周边
距离机场的车程分钟数、相邻地铁线路及出口、步行可达的商圈或地标，这些空间标签是AI做行程规划的基本参数。当企业差旅系统对接AI搜索时，酒店与客户办事处之间的步行距离甚至能成为筛选的硬指标。某产业园酒店补全“距会展中心步行8分钟”“地铁2号线创新园站C口出即达”等数据后，在商务推荐场景中被唤醒的频次明显上升。务必在主流地图和生活服务平台上完成位置认领，上传门头、大堂和路线视频。周边配套如“楼下有24小时便利店”“对街为本地人气餐厅”也要写进简介，确保AI能自如回答“酒店旁边有什么吃的”这类长尾提问，让酒店出现在更多即兴行程的备选清单。
资料四：企业客户与会议
会议与团队生意的获取，AI正成为企业秘书和行政采购的新入口。会议室数量、面积、可布台型、设备清单、茶歇标准、住宿打包价格，都要制成清晰文字，并附平面图和实拍视频链接。某长三角全服务酒店将“课桌式可容80人、LED屏尺寸、同声传译间数”毫无保留地公开后，通过AI协作工具导入的询价量在季度内实现扎实的增长。无须隐藏企业协议价逻辑，可用“提供长住优惠与协议公司定制服务”来示意。当用户指令是“找适合20人封闭培训的会场酒店”，算法若抓取不到你的会议参数，酒店瞬间就会出局。会议室写透，AI才敢替你开口。
资料五：真实评价与案例
AI评审一家酒店时，会综合多源口碑：OTA评价、社交平台笔记、本地生活点评、新闻案例。酒店要主动沉淀带图评价，并引导客人提及具体的服务触点，如“前台小周帮忙找回遗失的电脑”。这些细节会被算法当作信用的凭证。还可以整理成“住客故事”发布在官网上，例如“一对情侣在此完成星空求婚”“某研发小组长住三周完成产品冲刺”。故事里可以自然嵌入关键词：求婚策划、长住洗衣、24小时打印。某轻奢民宿在补齐多平台问答和客人案例后，针对“有求婚布置的民宿”这类搜索，排名大幅前移。口碑一旦丰满，AI的推荐胆量就会上升。
操作清单5件事
1. 制作一份信息主文档，统一客群标签、房型详细清单和交通指引，作为官网、百科与所有分发平台的标准信源底稿。
2. 在地图和生活服务APP上认领并完善位置信息，上传门头、大堂与周边路线实拍，确保坐标和入离指引无误。
3. 将会议设施与商务服务整理成结构化页面，设置官网一级入口，同步到企业信息与会议预订平台。
4. 每月定向邀约不同客群留下五条带图评价，覆盖亲子、会议、情侣等标签，并用心回复每条评论。
5. 每季度在本地媒体或行业网站上发布一篇客户案例，用故事包裹服务关键词，构建可被AI持续抓取的内容池。
4个FAQ
问：AI推荐引擎从哪里抓取酒店信息？
答：来自搜索引擎知识图谱、地图平台基础数据、OTA深层接口、官网结构化标记、百科词条、新闻源与多平台点评库。信息一致、维度越全，被整合进推荐结果的机会越大。
问：没有会议设施或企业客户案例该怎么写？
答：如实标注“无会议设施”，同时突出替代方案，如单人长住优惠、安静的客房办公环境、高速网络与打印机。AI会将其匹配发给自由职业者和移动办公者。虚构内容反易引发算法降权。
问：信息多久更新一次更合理？
答：基础信息每季度复核一次，季节活动提前一个月更新，硬件改造完成后一周内同步至所有主流平台。实时变动如满房和临时闭店，可用平台后台即时调整，防止错误信息沉淀。
问：补齐资料后多久能看到效果？
答：大模型知识库更新需数天到数周，通常两周后开始零星出现，一个月进入稳定召回期。持续累积口碑与互动信号，推荐位会不断巩固，效果随时间叠加放大。
了解更多，可访问 https://www.marvelbros.com
contentEn:
For Hotels to Be Recommended by AI, First Complete These Five Categories of Public Information
Hotels are not recommended by AI because they lack advertising copy. They are recommended when their public information lets the system and the customer judge who the hotel is for, what needs it solves, and why it is worth choosing. The first step is not writing more ads. It is completing five categories of public information: target guests, room and service scenarios, transport and surroundings, corporate client capability, and verified reviews.
An operating scene: a guest asks AI for hotels near the office that are suitable for business travel. The hotel has meeting rooms, good transport, and steady business occupancy. The hotel does not appear in the answer. The reason is not bad product. The reason is that the hotel's public information does not give AI the signals it needs to match this query.
Category 1: Target guest profile
AI matches queries to entities by scenario fit, not by location alone. A hotel must clearly state who it is for: business travelers, families, long-stay guests, meeting attendees, or leisure tourists. The statement must be specific, not generic. "Suitable for business travel" is too broad. "Suitable for business travelers on single-night transfers with quick check-in and 24-hour room service" gives AI a precise scenario to match.
Action: write a clear "who we are for" paragraph for each main guest type. Place this paragraph on the official site About page, OTA property description, and corporate profile.
Category 2: Room and service information
Traditional room descriptions focus on hardware: 35 sqm, king bed, city view. AI and modern guests need usage scenarios. The same king bed serves a business traveler and a honeymoon couple very differently. A room description should explain usage: "suitable for single-night business stays with ergonomic work desk and blackout curtains" or "suitable for couples with late check-out flexibility and quiet floor access".
Action: rewrite every room type description in scenario language, not just hardware language.
Category 3: Transport and surrounding information
Transport is a top-three decision factor for most travelers. But the information hotels provide is usually vague: "convenient transport" or "near the city center". AI needs structured facts: walking time to nearest metro station, driving time to airport, distance to nearest highway exit, parking capacity, EV charging availability. Surrounding information should also be in customer decision language: "10-minute walk to the international conference center" rather than "located in the central business district".
Action: produce a transport and surrounding page with concrete times, distances, and customer use cases. Avoid generic location descriptions.
Category 4: Corporate client and meeting information
Corporate clients and meeting organizers have specific B2B needs: invoicing capability, contract pricing, group booking process, meeting room specs with capacity and equipment, catering options, on-site support. AI queries about "hotels suitable for hosting a 50-person training session" need to find properties that explicitly state they can serve this scenario. Many hotels can serve this scenario in reality but do not state it publicly.
Action: build a corporate client page with invoicing terms, meeting room capacity, equipment list, catering menus, and contract process. Make this page easy to find and easy for AI to read.
Category 5: Verified reviews and case evidence
Reviews matter for both guests and AI. But unverified review summaries do not help AI understand what scenarios the hotel serves well. Case evidence, even short and anonymized, helps AI see specific use patterns: "hosted a 40-person industry association annual meeting with multi-track sessions and banquet". Verified evidence with concrete scenario details is more useful than aggregate review scores.
Action: collect 5 to 10 anonymized case studies showing specific guest scenarios and outcomes. Publish them on the official site with structured format.
How to verify whether the work is effective
After completing the five information categories, the hotel should not judge success only by page views. The first verification point is accuracy: when staff search the hotel name, core room types, and target scenarios in mainstream search and AI tools, the returned description should match the hotel's current facts. The second point is coverage: more long-tail questions should be answered by the hotel's own website, not only by OTA pages. The third point is inquiry quality: sales and front-office teams should receive fewer basic questions and more specific requests, such as meeting-room capacity, corporate billing, long-stay arrangements, or family facilities.
This verification should be repeated monthly. The general manager can assign one person to test ten fixed questions and record whether the official site appears, whether the answer is accurate, and whether outdated information still appears. If an old room policy or wrong facility description remains visible, update the source page first and then check the related OTA, map, and social profiles. GEO is not a one-time writing task. It is an operating discipline that keeps the hotel's public information aligned with real service capability.
Action checklist: 5 things general managers can do this week
1. Unify hotel name, address, and contact information across official site, OTA, maps, WeChat official account, and corporate profile. Eliminate variant names.
2. For each main guest type, write a "suitable for whom, not suitable for whom" paragraph and place it on the official site About page and OTA property description.
3. Rewrite all room type descriptions in scenario language, not just hardware language. Add usage scenarios to every room type.
4. Add explicit corporate client and meeting capability information: invoicing, contract pricing, meeting room specs, catering options, and booking process.
5. Collect 5 to 10 anonymized case studies of recent guest scenarios and publish them on the official site with structured format including guest type, scenario, services delivered, and outcome.
4 FAQs
1. Does doing GEO require rebuilding the official site? Not necessarily. A full rebuild is rarely needed. The priority is content completeness, structured format, and cross-platform consistency. Most hotels can complete the critical updates within the current site structure.
2. Our hotel is small with no technical team. Can we start with content only? Yes. Content and information architecture are the entry point. Schema markup and other technical layers can come later. Start with the five categories above.
3. OTA information is already complete. What else is needed? OTA covers price and basic description. What OTA does not cover is scenario depth, use cases, corporate capability, and verified case evidence. These categories need to live on the official site and in structured public documents.
4. What public information do corporate travel clients care about most? Invoicing capability, contract pricing transparency, meeting room specs, on-site response time, and case evidence of similar events hosted. Hotels that publish this information clearly will be selected more often in corporate travel system shortlists.
Closing
Digital transformation for hotels is not about replacing systems. It is about turning operating capability into recognizable, callable content assets. The five categories above are the foundation. Without them, no amount of marketing spend will make AI recommend the hotel in the right scenario.
MarvelBros C&T / 迈创兄弟C&T provides hotel investors and operating teams with operational diagnostics, AI implementation, and digital transformation consulting focused on GEO and AI visibility construction.
For more insights on hotel AI visibility and digital operations, visit https://www.marvelbros.com.
MarvelBros C&T / 迈创兄弟C&T
author: 迈创兄弟C&T（MarvelBros C&T）
